methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $107M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Heart Aerospace, a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Anders Forslund, has raised $107M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer universe of 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, and Sift—all at equivalent stage.

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $107M raised proxy, implying $203M. Peer-median valuation across four same-sector, same-stage private companies yields $700M, with a range of $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 45.3x across 6 tickers) cannot be applied without target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $173M to $805M, with a median of $452M across the two applicable methods. Stage-progression anchors the low end, reflecting a conservative capital-raised proxy approach, while peer-median anchors the high end given Heart Aerospace's competitive positioning within the Space & Aerospace cohort. Key uncertainties include: the proprietary revenue trajectory and margins relative to named peers, exact progress toward Series C readiness, and whether the peer sample adequately reflects market risk pricing for early-stage aerospace ventures.
